hero.subheadline: Shared GPUs. Open-source models. Closed community.
hero.description: Builders sharing GPUs to run the latest open AI models without token limits.

about.label: // what is nan
about.heading: Builders sharing dedicated GPUs to run open models and burn tokens without limits.
about.p1: Closed-model APIs are convenient but expensive and limited. Open models are free but running them seriously requires hardware almost no one has at home.
about.p2: NaN solves this by sharing powerful GPUs among builders who actually use them, splitting the real cost of infrastructure.
about.p3: A closed community by design, limited to the real capacity of shared GPUs. When it fills up, we open a waitlist.
about.p4: The community lives on Discord. The website only manages access. Every member pays to be here. Only those who truly want to build make it in.

server.label: // inference cluster
server.heading: Inference cluster
server.description: Shared inference cluster. Models chosen by the community every quarter.

guarantees.0: Your code doesn't train any models
guarantees.1: Data in EU, no prompt logs
guarantees.3: Personal API key for each member

privacy.label: // privacy
privacy.heading: Pure inference. Zero logs.
privacy.p1: NaN is inference infrastructure. There is no logging of prompts, model responses, nothing. Everything lives in your local session, on whatever client, agent, or custom app you use.
privacy.p2: The API is pure inference. Nothing you do with it is logged except server-side metrics (tokens/s, RPM, etc.) for cluster maintenance and monitoring only.
privacy.p3: Your code doesn't train any models. Your prompts aren't stored. Your data doesn't leave your session.

points.noLogs: Zero logs: no prompt or response logging
points.localData: Everything lives in your local client or agent session
points.noTraining: Your code doesn't train any models
points.metrics: Server metrics only for cluster maintenance (tokens/s, RPM)

modelVoting.label: // how models are chosen
modelVoting.heading: Models chosen by the community. Refreshed every quarter.

rules.0: Every 3 months, members vote on the new models that will run on the cluster.
rules.1: Candidates are pre-selected technically: they must fit in the cluster capacity.
rules.2: Voting happens on Discord, open to all members.
rules.3: The open models ecosystem moves fast. So does NaN.

agents.label: // agents platform
agents.heading: Your own microVM. Root access. KVM-isolated.
agents.description: Beyond the API. Every member can deploy an agent into its own QEMU-isolated microVM — full access to the cluster's models, a web terminal, file uploads, and native observability.

0.name: Isolated microVM
0.desc: QEMU/KVM-isolated microVM. Own kernel, own FS, root with no risk to the host.

1.name: Hermes preinstalled
1.desc: Conversational agent ready to go. Plug in a Telegram token and start chatting.

2.name: Web terminal
2.desc: Interactive console in the browser. apt, pip, git — everything persists.

3.name: File uploads
3.desc: Drag-and-drop up to 200 MiB per file. Datasets, PDFs, configs.

4.name: Native observability
4.desc: Live logs, K8s events, CPU/RAM/disk metrics — nothing to install.

5.name: Public HTTP endpoint
5.desc: Expose any of the agent's web services at a *.apps.nan.builders URL.

guarantees.0: Full root access inside your microVM.
guarantees.1: Real persistence: apt installs, configs, data — all survives restarts.
guarantees.3: Kernel-level isolation from the host and from other members.

founder.label: // who's behind
founder.heading: The community is run by one person. Not a company.

bio.0: Founder of Helmcode, working with startups on cloud infrastructure, AI tooling and open source.
bio.1: Thanks to Helmcode we offer open model inference with unlimited token plans for enterprises, I share my experiments on social media and AI research.

founder.quote: I started NaN because I wanted to run serious open models without usage limits, and share and learn with people in the same boat: builders who actually burn tokens.

pricing.label: // pricing
pricing.subheading: One tier per region. No upsells. No trial.

features.0: Access to shared cluster — open models with no token caps
features.1: DeepSeek V4-Flash (frontier 284B, 1M context, advanced reasoning) — 500M tokens/month
features.2: Open models chosen by the community
features.3: Personal API key compatible with OpenAI
features.4: Private Discord channels for members only

communityFeatures.3: Reserved spot in line for inference

pricing.footerNote: Limited spots. When it fills up, waitlist.

cta.label: // join
cta.heading: A community of builders. Limited spots. Shared GPUs with unlimited usage.
cta.description: We share dedicated GPUs to run open models and burn tokens without limits. Leave your email and we'll reach out when we open payments.

0.question: What is NaN?
0.answer: NaN is a closed community of builders who share access to dedicated GPUs to run open-source AI models. Every member pays to be here, and there's only room for those who will actually build.

1.question: How does the cluster work?
1.answer: Each community member shares a dedicated inference cluster. Cluster models run 24/7 and each member gets a personal OpenAI-compatible API key to use the models as they want, with no token caps.

2.question: Are there really no token limits?
2.answer: You can burn tokens without limits on the smaller cluster models. Frontier models (like DeepSeek V4-Flash) come with a generous monthly token allowance per member to keep the plan sustainable. No surprises on the bill: the flat subscription price is all you pay.

3.question: How are models chosen?
3.answer: Every quarter, community members vote on which new models will run on the cluster. Candidates are pre-selected technically to ensure they fit within the cluster capacity. Voting happens on Discord, open to all members.

4.question: Where does my data go?
4.answer: Data is processed in the European Union, with no prompt logging. Your API key is personal and non-transferable. We don't share data between members or sell information to third parties. Your code doesn't train any models.

5.question: Who is NaN for?
5.answer: For builders who want to run serious AI models without the overhead of managing infrastructure. If you're a startup, researcher, or maker who needs inference at scale without token limits, NaN is for you. It's not for casual experimentation.

6.question: Why only open models?
6.answer: Because we believe real innovation comes from tools you can audit, modify, and use without restrictions. Closed models are convenient but create dependency. With open models you have full control over your stack.

7.question: Can I train models?
7.answer: No. NaN is for inference, not training. The GPUs are optimized to run existing models at high speed, not for fine-tuning. If you need training, there are other platforms for that.

8.question: What payment methods do you accept?
8.answer: We currently accept credit/debit cards. Prices include taxes (VAT for EU, taxes for USA/Latam). You can cancel anytime, no commitment.

9.question: How do I join the community?
9.answer: Leave your email on the waitlist and we'll reach out when we open payments. The community lives on Discord — once you join, you get access to all private channels, quarterly votes, and direct support from the team.

waitlist.followUp: We don't send newsletters. We'll only write when we open payments.

confirmation.label: // confirmation
confirmation.heading: Are you sure you want to join?
confirmation.p1: You're joining the waitlist for a paid private community. When we open payments, a monthly subscription will be required to access shared GPUs and the models chosen by the community.
confirmation.p2: We only want people with real intention to pay to join. If you're just curious, better not join.

waitlist.footer: No spam. No newsletters. We only write when we open payments.

waitlistHelpers.successInterest: Noted. We're available for ${regionLabel}. We'll let you know as soon as we open payments.
waitlistHelpers.successRegistered: You're on the list. We'll accept members in order of arrival when we open payments.

errors.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Wait a minute.
errors.serverError: Something went wrong on the server. Try again in a moment.

community.label: // community
community.title: Join the community while you wait for an inference seat.
community.subtitle: Discord access, conversations with builders, and a reserved spot in line for GPU access when capacity frees up.
community.faqUpgradeQ: How do I move to the inference plan later?
community.faqUpgradeA: When GPU capacity frees up, we'll invite you manually. In the meantime you can request the upgrade from your member dashboard.
community.faqCancelA: Yes, anytime from your member dashboard. No commitment.
community.alreadySubscribed: You're already in. If you didn't get a receipt, write to us at
community.errorRateLimited: Too many attempts. Wait a minute.
community.errorServer: Something went wrong. Try again in a moment.

hackaton.label: // hackathon
hackaton.title: #1 NaN Hackathon
hackaton.subtitle: 7 days to build with the cluster's open source models. Teams of 4, balanced. Prize: 1 month of NaN free.
hackaton.dates: June 7–13 · build week · Sunday 14th: Demo Day
hackaton.rules: Allowed models: Qwen 3.6 or Gemma 4. The project must be open source. Free theme.
